-- ============================================================
-- Luna HRM ƒ?" Migration 002: Row-Level Security Policies
-- Role hierarchy: admin > branch_manager > accountant > employee
-- Roles stored in auth.jwt()->'app_metadata' (NOT user_metadata)
-- ============================================================

-- ============================================================
-- HELPER FUNCTIONS
-- Read role and branch_id from JWT app_metadata (immutable by client)
-- ============================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_user_role()
RETURNS TEXT AS $$
  SELECT (auth.jwt()->'app_metadata'->>'role')::text;
$$ LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_user_branch_id()
RETURNS UUID AS $$
  SELECT (auth.jwt()->'app_metadata'->>'branch_id')::uuid;
$$ LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER;

-- ============================================================
-- ENABLE RLS on all 17 tables
-- ============================================================
ALTER TABLE branches               ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE employees              ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE class_schedules        ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE attendance             ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE office_attendance      ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE attendance_locks       ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE employee_weekly_notes  ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE kpi_evaluations        ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE payroll_periods        ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE payslips               ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE salary_components      ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE evaluation_templates   ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE evaluation_criteria    ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE evaluation_periods     ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE employee_evaluations   ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE evaluation_scores      ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE employee_notes         ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- ============================================================
-- TABLE: branches
-- Admin: full CRUD all branches
-- BM: SELECT/UPDATE own branch only
-- Accountant: SELECT all
-- Employee: SELECT own branch (via employee.branch_id)
-- ============================================================

CREATE POLICY "branches_admin_all" ON branches
  FOR ALL TO authenticated
  USING (get_user_role() = 'admin')
  WITH CHECK (get_user_role() = 'admin');

CREATE POLICY "branches_bm_select_own" ON branches
  FOR SELECT TO authenticated
  USING (get_user_role() = 'branch_manager' AND id = get_user_branch_id());

CREATE POLICY "branches_bm_update_own" ON branches
  FOR UPDATE TO authenticated
  USING (get_user_role() = 'branch_manager' AND id = get_user_branch_id())
  WITH CHECK (get_user_role() = 'branch_manager' AND id = get_user_branch_id());

CREATE POLICY "branches_accountant_select" ON branches
  FOR SELECT TO authenticated
  USING (get_user_role() = 'accountant');

CREATE POLICY "branches_employee_select_own" ON branches
  FOR SELECT TO authenticated
  USING (
    get_user_role() = 'employee'
    AND id = (SELECT branch_id FROM employees WHERE id = auth.uid())
  );

-- Accountant: SELECT all employees

34:}) {

36:
37:  // Create auth user

45:    },
46:  })
47:
48:  if (authError) throw authError

1:-- ============================================================
2:-- Luna HRM — Migration 001: Create All Tables
3:-- Order respects FK dependencies (staged circular FK for branches/employees)
4:-- ============================================================
5:
6:-- Enable UUID extension
7:CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pgcrypto";
8:
9:-- ============================================================
10:-- TRIGGER FUNCTION: auto-update updated_at on any row change
11:-- ============================================================
12:CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column()
13:RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
14:BEGIN
15:  NEW.updated_at = now();
16:  RETURN NEW;
17:END;
18:$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

20:-- ============================================================
21:-- TABLE 1: branches
22:-- Created WITHOUT manager_id FK first (staged circular FK)
23:-- manager_id FK added later via ALTER TABLE after employees exists
24:-- ============================================================
25:CREATE TABLE branches (
26:  id          UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
27:  name        TEXT        NOT NULL,
28:  address     TEXT,
29:  phone       TEXT,
30:  manager_id  UUID,       -- FK added below after employees is created
31:  is_active   BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
32:  created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
33:  updated_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
34:);
35:
36:CREATE TRIGGER trg_branches_updated_at
37:  BEFORE UPDATE ON branches
38:  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();

40:-- ============================================================
41:-- TABLE 2: employees
42:-- FK → branches. Includes full extended profile fields.
43:-- ============================================================
44:CREATE TABLE employees (
45:  id                  UUID        PRIMARY KEY,  -- same UUID as auth.users.id
46:  branch_id           UUID        REFERENCES branches(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
47:  employee_code       TEXT        NOT NULL,
48:  full_name           TEXT        NOT NULL,
49:  email               TEXT        NOT NULL,
50:  phone               TEXT,
51:  position            TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (position IN ('teacher', 'assistant', 'office', 'admin')),
52:  role                TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'branch_manager', 'accountant', 'employee')),
53:  rate_per_session    BIGINT      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
54:  sub_rate            BIGINT      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
55:  has_labor_contract  BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
56:  dependent_count     INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (dependent_count >= 0),
57:  is_active           BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
58:  join_date           DATE,
59:  -- Extended profile fields
60:  date_of_birth       DATE,
61:  id_number           TEXT,                    -- CCCD / CMND / Passport
62:  id_issue_date       DATE,
63:  id_issue_place      TEXT,
64:  address             TEXT,
65:  emergency_contact   TEXT,                    -- "Nguyen Van A — 0901234567"
66:  bank_account_number TEXT,
67:  bank_name           TEXT,
68:  nationality         TEXT,                    -- important for foreign teachers
69:  qualifications      TEXT,                    -- "IELTS 8.0, CELTA, TESOL"
70:  teaching_license    TEXT,
71:  characteristics     TEXT,                    -- BM notes: personality, strengths
72:  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
73:  updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
74:  UNIQUE (branch_id, employee_code)
75:);
76:
77:-- Case-insensitive unique email index
78:CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_employees_email_ci ON employees (lower(email));

80:CREATE TRIGGER trg_employees_updated_at
81:  BEFORE UPDATE ON employees
82:  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();

85:-- STAGED CIRCULAR FK: branches.manager_id → employees(id)
86:-- Now that employees table exists, add the FK constraint
87:-- ============================================================
88:ALTER TABLE branches
89:  ADD CONSTRAINT fk_branches_manager
90:  FOREIGN KEY (manager_id) REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;

93:-- TABLE 3: class_schedules
94:-- FK → branches, employees. CHECK teacher_id <> assistant_id.
95:-- ============================================================
96:CREATE TABLE class_schedules (
97:  id           UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
98:  branch_id    UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES branches(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
99:  class_code   TEXT        NOT NULL,
100:  class_name   TEXT        NOT NULL,
101:  shift_time   TEXT        NOT NULL,           -- "19:00-20:30"
102:  days_of_week INT[]       NOT NULL,           -- [2,4,6] = Mon,Wed,Fri
103:  teacher_id   UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
104:  assistant_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
105:  status       TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active', 'inactive')),
106:  created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
107:  updated_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
108:  UNIQUE (branch_id, class_code),
109:  CONSTRAINT chk_teacher_ne_assistant CHECK (teacher_id <> assistant_id),
110:  CONSTRAINT chk_days_of_week CHECK (
111:    days_of_week <@ ARRAY[1,2,3,4,5,6,7]::INT[]
112:  )
113:);

185:  description   TEXT        NOT NULL,

187:  amount_unit   TEXT        CHECK (amount_unit IN ('sessions', 'vnd')),

203:CREATE TABLE kpi_evaluations (
204:  id               UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
205:  employee_id      UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
206:  branch_id        UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES branches(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
207:  month            INT         NOT NULL CHECK (month BETWEEN 1 AND 12),
208:  year             INT         NOT NULL CHECK (year >= 2020),
209:  base_pass        BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT false,  -- 4 mandatory criteria met?
210:  tsi_score        INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (tsi_score BETWEEN 0 AND 1),
211:  tsi_comment      TEXT,
212:  funtime_score    INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (funtime_score BETWEEN 0 AND 3),
213:  funtime_comment  TEXT,
214:  parent_score     INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (parent_score BETWEEN 0 AND 2),
215:  parent_comment   TEXT,
216:  student_score    INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (student_score BETWEEN 0 AND 3),
217:  student_comment  TEXT,
218:  demeanor_score   INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (demeanor_score BETWEEN 0 AND 1),
219:  demeanor_comment TEXT,
220:  total_score      INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,     -- computed: sum of all scores (0-10)
221:  bonus_amount     BIGINT      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,     -- total_score × 50000 (0 if base_pass=false)
222:  evaluated_by     UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
223:  created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
224:  updated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
225:  UNIQUE (employee_id, month, year)

item.text:
### ISSUE-1: Branch managers can mint or promote privileged accounts
- Category: security
- Severity: critical
- Problem: `branch_manager` is allowed to create and update employees, but the server never constrains the target `role`. A forged server-action call can create or promote users to `admin` or `accountant`, and the service-role helper writes that role into `app_metadata`, which is what RLS trusts.
- Evidence: [lib/actions/employee-actions.ts:121](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/employee-actions.ts#L121) accepts any `input.role`; [lib/actions/employee-actions.ts:127](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/employee-actions.ts#L127) passes it to `createAuthUser`; [lib/actions/employee-actions.ts:201](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/employee-actions.ts#L201) syncs arbitrary `data.role` to auth metadata; [supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql:93](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql#L93) and [supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql:97](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql#L97) only check `branch_id`, not `role`.
- Suggested fix: On the server, reject any non-admin attempt to set or change `role`; for branch managers, hard-force allowed roles. Mirror that constraint in RLS `WITH CHECK`.

### ISSUE-2: Employees can rewrite their own authority fields, and app auth trusts the table
- Category: security
- Severity: critical
- Problem: Employees can update their own `employees` row with no column restrictions. Because `getCurrentUser()` derives `role` and `branch_id` from that row instead of JWT `app_metadata`, a normal employee can self-edit those fields and bypass application-level role checks. They can also pivot branch-scoped reads that depend on `employees.branch_id`.
- Evidence: [supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql:110](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql#L110) allows self-update on `employees` with no column guard; [lib/actions/auth-actions.ts:112](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/auth-actions.ts#L112) and [lib/actions/auth-actions.ts:123](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/auth-actions.ts#L123) return `role`/`branch_id` from `employees`; [supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql:69](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql#L69) and [supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql:234](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql#L234) derive employee branch access from the `employees` table.
- Suggested fix: Remove direct self-update on authority fields, or replace it with a narrow RPC/profile-update path for safe columns only. For authorization, trust JWT `app_metadata` or explicitly verify table role/branch matches JWT before granting privileged actions.

### ISSUE-3: Auth metadata and employee rows can drift because create/update is non-atomic
- Category: security
- Severity: high
- Problem: Auth-side writes and table-side writes are split across separate calls with no rollback. Failures leave orphaned auth users or role/branch mismatches, and the app/RLS already read authority from different sources.
- Evidence: [lib/actions/employee-actions.ts:126](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/employee-actions.ts#L126) creates the auth user before the `employees` insert at [lib/actions/employee-actions.ts:139](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/employee-actions.ts#L139); [lib/actions/employee-actions.ts:195](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/employee-actions.ts#L195) updates the row before syncing auth metadata at [lib/actions/employee-actions.ts:201](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/employee-actions.ts#L201); [lib/supabase/admin.ts:55](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/supabase/admin.ts#L55) performs the metadata update separately; [lib/actions/auth-actions.ts:123](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/auth-actions.ts#L123) uses table role while RLS uses JWT role from [supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql:12](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql#L12).
- Suggested fix: Move employee creation/update into one trusted backend unit with compensation or rollback. At minimum, delete the newly created auth user if the row insert fails, and abort or revert the row update if metadata sync fails.

### ISSUE-4: Server-action authorization does not match the declared RLS model
- Category: bug
- Severity: medium
- Problem: The TypeScript layer blocks operations that the SQL policy layer explicitly allows, so the app and database no longer implement the same authorization contract.
- Evidence: [lib/actions/employee-actions.ts:46](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/employee-actions.ts#L46) excludes accountants from employee listing, but [supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql:102](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql#L102) allows accountant select; [lib/actions/branch-actions.ts:29](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/branch-actions.ts#L29) and [lib/actions/branch-actions.ts:90](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/lib/actions/branch-actions.ts#L90) deny accountant branch reads and branch-manager branch updates, while [supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql:60](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql#L60) and [supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql:65](/F:/APP ANTIGRAVITY/Tool/Luna HRM/luna-hrm/supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql#L65) allow them.
- Suggested fix: Pick one source of truth for permissions. Either align the server-action guards to the RLS design or remove the unused policies so expected behavior is unambiguous.

### VERDICT
- Status: REVISE
- Reason: There are two critical privilege-escalation paths around employee role/branch authority, plus a high-risk auth/table consistency problem. The current implementation is not safe to approve as-is.
